items.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine gets green light to challenge China's dominance

children.content: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

gallery.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

thumbnail.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

items.title: Defence minister 'disappointed' by lawsuit over Darwin port ownership

children.content: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.

image.altText: White man standing in front of podium, light pink shirt, navy blue pants, mid-speech, man behind him in white shirt.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' failure to attend Aboriginal housing talks

children.content: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

image.altText: A woman with curly brown hair and in a brown collared shirt looks at the camera

items.title: NT Health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback

children.content: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

articles.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A doctor with his arms folded stands in front of a window

articles.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

articles.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

gallery.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

items.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

image.altText: Albo has arms around an Indigenous woman wearing scarf, holding pink toy, a woman has head on another's shoulder. All serious.

items.title: WHO-linked report warns world more at risk of pandemic now than before COVID

children.content: As health leaders face deadly outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, a major report finds the world's response system remains dangerously underprepared.

image.altText: Crowd of people wearing face masks walking across a scramble crossing in Brisbane CBD.

items.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rallies

children.content: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

image.altText: an aerial photo of a mining processing plant.

items.title: NT Police officer acquitted of coercing ex-partner over T-shirt assault

children.content: A Northern Territory Police officer has been cleared of coercing his former partner into withdrawing an assault charge after facing trial on one count of attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A group of people in suits walking out of the NT Supreme Court.

items.title: How a 'partial obscuration' phenomenon is impacting Darwin's sunsets

children.content: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

gallery.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Large clouds obscure the setting sun, casting shadows of blue sky over the rays of sunlight.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

thumbnail.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Large clouds obscure the setting sun, casting shadows of blue sky over the rays of sunlight.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

items.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away

children.content: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.

image.altText: An aerial view of Milingimbi community in north-east Arnhem Land.

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

image.altText: Aboriginal woman, brown/gray hair tied back, blue pattern deress, serious expression, sitting, blurred greenery behind her

items.title: Coles investigates 'weird scratching' Apple gift card scam complaint

children.content: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

image.altText: A close up of an Apple gift card with the numbers scratched off.

items.title: How Australia's EV uptake compares with the rest of the world

children.content: High petrol prices are driving EV sales around the world, with the leading international authority predicting 30 per cent of all new cars sold this year will be electric.

image.altText: workers in a car factory

items.title: Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure

children.content: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

articles.title: NRL star Arrow reveals MND has already affected his speech

children.content: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

items.title: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Abetz looks to rein in Tasmanian spending

children.content: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

image.altText: Treasurer Eric Abetz in front of a budget cuts sign and piles of money.

children.content: A 71-year-old woman has died after being hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.

items.title: As Colbert wraps up The Late Show, late-night TV could be on shaky ground

children.content: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

articles.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the US's most important satirists

children.content: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

image.altText: Stephen Colbert sits behind a desk on the Late Show set

items.title: Fears CSIRO job cuts will damage Australia's climate change adaptability

children.content: Peak body Science and Technology Australia is concerned the CSIRO's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.

children.content: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

items.title: Former NSW Young Australian of the Year dies aged 32

children.content: Nikhil Autar, who was diagnosed with leukaemia when he was 17 years old, was named NSW Young Australian of the Year in 2024.

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

items.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from notorious paedophile

children.content: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.

items.title: Rockhampton hotel fallen into disrepair sells for $5.75 million at auction

children.content: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

items.title: Iran examining US proposal as Trump says talks in 'final stages'
